page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i5.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.4 GHz. The instance features 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, providing 959 GB of local SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, and virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

The inclusion of 959 GB of local SSD storage alongside a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a resource-dense option for workloads requiring fast local data access and moderate memory capacity. Because the CPU allocation is dedicated with a single thread per physical core, the instance avoids resource contention issues common in multi-threaded or shared environments. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for machine learning training or complex rendering tasks. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bundled local SSD storage reduces the need for external block storage attachments, optimizing the resource-to-cost ratio for database hosting, caching layers, and localized data processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i5.xlarge is an x86_64 server instance utilizing a dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor clocked at 3.4 GHz. It features 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, delivering 4 dedicated vCPUs alongside 32.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes a bundled 959 GB local SSD storage drive and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps, operating on the KVM hypervisor. With an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides predictable compute performance and high local storage capacity. It is designed for workloads such as database hosting, caching layers, and localized data processing where fast local storage and moderate memory density are required. The lack of GPU acceleration makes it less suitable for parallel computing or deep learning tasks.
